title: t-STEP: An interpretable model for Total Electron Content predictions and irregularities estimations
summary:
arXiv:2606.29644v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Earth system infrastructures relying on satellite-based technologies, such as Global Positioning System (GPS) communications, are affected by ionospheric Total Electron Content (TEC) gradients. Modeling these gradients under physical constraints remains challenging due to their dynamic and transient nature. While existing machine learning (ML) models can predict hourly TEC variations, it remains unclear whether their temporal resolution is sufficient to preserve small-scale TEC irregularities within predicted signals. title: Excursion-set structure factor of the auroral electric field
summary:
arXiv:2606.26854v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: We treat coherent radar echoes from aurorae as a finite point process and measure its structure factor $S(k)$ from pairwise echo separations. Backscatter requires electron drifts to exceed the ion-acoustic speed, making the echoes a threshold (excursion-set) sample of the ionospheric electric field, and $|S-1|$ is that field's spectrum, to leading order.
